global_rules: All exam diagrams must be local files before PDF export.
Use black-and-white textbook or workbook line art suitable for printing.
Do not put long question text inside images; keep question text in HTML.
Keep only necessary labels such as A/B/C/D, 图 1, 图 2, axes, scale marks, or simple place names.
Figures must be readable after A4 PDF export; generated HTML must not render figures below 160 CSS px wide.
If a generated diagram is reused as a stable sample, commit it under templates/exam-paper/assets or templates/exam-paper/mobile/science-assets.

For source-photo reconstruction papers, the source figure is the contract: preserve the original figure meaning, object count, grouping, labels, data values, axis names, relative positions, and experiment-control relationships.
Do not let the image model invent a new diagram, add explanatory text, omit key objects, simplify controlled experiments, or change table/graph values.
Only optimize line clarity, print contrast, alignment, and A4 readability when those changes do not alter the original question intent.
Text inside reconstructed source figures must be limited to text present in the original figure and must remain in Chinese where the original uses Chinese.
Before delivery, visually compare each reconstructed figure against its source photo or a locked detail manifest; regenerate any figure whose key details do not match.
knowledge_explanation_rules: Knowledge-point explanations should be illustrated when the concept is visual, spatial, experimental, structural, map-based, chart-based, or process-based.
For math, illustrate geometry, coordinate systems, statistics charts, transformations, and multi-step word-problem models.
For science, illustrate apparatus, biological structures, matter models, physical processes, charts, and experiment variables.
For English, illustrate maps, timetables, routes, classroom scenes, and reading contexts when they help comprehension.
Keep diagrams next to the matching knowledge point or question, not in a separate image appendix.

math.required_style: clean black-and-white junior-middle-school math workbook diagrams

figures.prompt: Black-and-white coordinate plane, triangle ABC and translated triangle A'B'C', grid lines, clear axis arrows and point labels, no long text.
Black-and-white geometry diagram, two parallel horizontal lines cut by a transversal, angles labeled 1 and 2, clean workbook style.
Black-and-white line chart for weekly reading counts, x-axis Monday to Friday, y-axis counts, clear markers and grid, no decorative style.

science.required_style: compressed black-and-white science textbook line diagrams for mobile-safe PDF

figures.prompt: Black-and-white microscope structure diagram, labels A/B/C/D on eyepiece, objective lens, stage, mirror or light source, clean textbook line art.
Black-and-white flower structure diagram, labels for petal, stamen, pistil, ovary with A/B/C/D markers, clean junior science style.
Black-and-white atom model, central nucleus with proton and neutron symbols, electrons around shell, simple labels A/B/C/D.
Black-and-white temperature-time heating curve, plateau segment visible, axis labels temperature and time, simple grid.
Black-and-white food chain diagram, grass to insect to frog to snake to hawk, arrows clear, labels A/B/C/D/E only.
Black-and-white soil profile cross-section, topsoil, subsoil, parent material, roots, rock layer, labels A/B/C.
Black-and-white root tip diagram, root cap, meristem, elongation zone, root hair zone, simple labels A/B/C/D.
Black-and-white graduated cylinder before and after adding stone, water levels V1 and V2, clean lab apparatus line art.
Black-and-white seed germination experiment setup, dry cotton, moist cotton, flooded condition, labels A/B/C, no long text.

english.required_style: clear black-and-white reading-comprehension map or scene

figures.prompt: Black-and-white simple town map for junior English reading comprehension, school, library, bank, park, supermarket, roads and compass, labels large enough to read.
